description:
Your role is to play the “backup-operator” for a small project that lives entirely inside the normal, non-privileged user account “user”.  
    The goal is to demonstrate a very simple backup / restore / verify cycle, while simultaneously showing that you understand how to manage **file-system
    permissions** that a real-world backup operator would care about.

    1. Workspace layout that **must** exist when you are finished  
       • /home/user/workspace/secure_data            –– the live data to be protected  
       • /home/user/workspace/backups                –– where compressed backups are deposited  
       • /home/user/workspace/restore_test           –– scratch space used to test a restore  

    2. Data that **must** exist in the live data directory  
       Inside /home/user/workspace/secure_data create two plain-text files with the exact names, exact letter-case and exact contents shown below.  
       a) /home/user/workspace/secure_data/file1.txt  
          Content (including the trailing newline):  
          Alpha line\n  
       b) /home/user/workspace/secure_data/file2.txt  
          Content (including the trailing newline):  
          Beta line\n  

    3. Permission model you have to implement  
       • /home/user/workspace/secure_data :  700  
       • every *.txt file inside it       :  600  
       • /home/user/workspace/backups     :  750  
       • the backup archive itself        :  640  
       • /home/user/workspace/restore_test:  700  
       • files produced during the restore:  600  

       (Use regular chmod and chown commands; no root privileges are needed because everything is already owned by “user”.)

    4. Backup step  
       • Create a gzip-compressed tar archive named  
         /home/user/workspace/backups/secure_data_backup_20230115.tar.gz  
         (the literal date string “20230115” is hard-coded so automated tests are deterministic).  
       • The archive must contain **only** the two *.txt files, not the parent directory itself.  
       • Make sure the archive’s permissions match what is listed in step 3.

    5. Restore test step  
       • Extract the archive you just created into /home/user/workspace/restore_test.  
       • After extraction the two restored files must be found at  
         /home/user/workspace/restore_test/file1.txt  
         /home/user/workspace/restore_test/file2.txt  
         with the permissions given in step 3 and with byte-for-byte identical contents.

    6. Manual verification step  
       • Perform a comparison (for example with diff or cmp) that proves the restored files are identical to the originals.

    7. Mandatory log file  
       When everything is done, create a three-line, plain-text log file at  
       /home/user/backup_restore.log  
       The file must contain **exactly** the following three lines and nothing else
       (three lines, each terminated with a single newline, no trailing blank line):

       20230115T000000Z BACKUP  SUCCESS Archive /home/user/workspace/backups/secure_data_backup_20230115.tar.gz  
       20230115T000000Z RESTORE SUCCESS Extracted to /home/user/workspace/restore_test  
       20230115T000000Z VERIFY  SUCCESS Restored data matches original  

       The spacing, upper-case keywords and paths must match exactly so that automated tests can validate the result with a strict string comparison.

    8. What the automated grader will check  
       • Presence, permissions (via stat), and contents of every file & directory named above.  
       • Existence of the exact archive with the right internal path layout.  
       • Byte-for-byte equality of original and restored data.  
       • Exact content of /home/user/backup_restore.log.

    Work strictly inside /home/user – you have no root privileges.  
    Show useful console output if you wish, but remember the grader looks only at the filesystem state and the log file.
truth:
<!--
        Ground-truth that the grader will use.
        DO NOT reveal any of this under the public <task> section!
    -->

    final_directories:
        - path: /home/user/workspace/secure_data
          mode: "700"
        - path: /home/user/workspace/backups
          mode: "750"
        - path: /home/user/workspace/restore_test
          mode: "700"

    final_files:
        - path: /home/user/workspace/secure_data/file1.txt
          mode: "600"
          content: "Alpha line\n"
        - path: /home/user/workspace/secure_data/file2.txt
          mode: "600"
          content: "Beta line\n"
        - path: /home/user/workspace/backups/secure_data_backup_20230115.tar.gz
          mode: "640"
          archive_internals:
              - name: file1.txt
                content: "Alpha line\n"
              - name: file2.txt
                content: "Beta line\n"
        - path: /home/user/workspace/restore_test/file1.txt
          mode: "600"
          content: "Alpha line\n"
        - path: /home/user/workspace/restore_test/file2.txt
          mode: "600"
          content: "Beta line\n"
        - path: /home/user/backup_restore.log
          mode: "644"
          content: |
              20230115T000000Z BACKUP  SUCCESS Archive /home/user/workspace/backups/secure_data_backup_20230115.tar.gz
              20230115T000000Z RESTORE SUCCESS Extracted to /home/user/workspace/restore_test
              20230115T000000Z VERIFY  SUCCESS Restored data matches original
